agent.name: Content Writer
agent.summary: Writes blog posts, docs, and marketing copy with optional research.
agent.description: Generates polished written content in the requested style and format.

input: Write a 3-paragraph summary about what Claude Agent SDK is and why developers use it. Keep it under 300 words.
inputPreview: Write a 3-paragraph summary about what Claude Agent SDK is and why developers use it. Keep it under 300 words.
output:
# Claude Agent SDK: A Developer's Overview

The Claude Agent SDK is Anthropic's framework for building autonomous AI agents powered by Claude. Rather than simply sending one-off prompts to a language model, the SDK enables developers to construct agents that can reason across multi-step tasks, use tools, delegate work to sub-agents, and maintain context throughout complex workflows. It provides the scaffolding needed to move beyond single-turn interactions into persistent, goal-directed AI behavior.

Developers adopt the Claude Agent SDK because it dramatically reduces the engineering overhead of building reliable agent systems. The SDK handles the coordination layer — managing tool calls, orchestrating parallel and sequential sub-tasks, and routing information between agents — so teams can focus on product logic rather than infrastructure. Built-in support for tool use, memory patterns, and structured outputs means common agent capabilities come out of the box instead of being hand-rolled for every project.

The SDK also appeals to developers who prioritize safety and predictability. Because it is built on Claude's foundation, agents inherit Anthropic's Constitutional AI principles, making them better suited for production environments where harmful or erratic behavior carries real risk. Whether building customer support automation, coding assistants, research pipelines, or data-processing workflows, the Claude Agent SDK gives developers a principled, scalable path from prototype to deployment — without sacrificing control over how their agents think and act.

---
*Based on Anthropic's published documentation and SDK design as of March 2026.*
